The composition is tight and slightly low-angled, placing the viewer at eye level with the goblin and intensifying the sense of confrontation and immediacy. The goblin’s physique is muscular and compact, with thick, sinewy arms and rough, mottled green skin covered in grime, scars, and subtle variations in tone—olive, dark jade, and earthy undertones—suggesting years of brutal combat and survival in harsh environments.

His face is highly expressive and deeply textured, with exaggerated goblin features: a wide, flattened nose, pronounced brow ridges, sharp cheekbones, and large, pointed ears that extend outward. His eyes glow faintly amber-yellow, piercing and focused, conveying aggression, alertness, and a predator-like intensity. His lips curl slightly around a lit cigarette clenched between his teeth, with thin wisps of smoke rising and curling into the humid jungle air. The cigarette ember glows faintly orange, adding a small but vivid point of contrast against the cooler tones of his skin.

Across his bare upper torso runs a heavy bandolier of large-calibre ammunition, the brass casings catching highlights from nearby firelight. The background is a chaotic jungle battlefield, dense with tall, dark tree trunks and thick undergrowth. Sparks and embers float through the air, frozen mid-motion, adding energy and dynamism to the scene. Smoke billows upward in thick, semi-transparent plumes, diffusing the light and creating a hazy, cinematic atmosphere.

Lighting is dramatic and high-contrast, with strong directional firelight illuminating one side of the goblin’s face and body, while cooler ambient light from the jungle fills in the shadows. prompt:
Authentic color CCTV security camera footage from a ceiling-mounted wide-angle dome camera in the corner of a small convenience store, captured at 2:47 AM. The camera is mounted high near the ceiling looking sharply downward at a steep overhead angle across the service counter and front area of the store — the flattened top-down fish-eye distortion of a real commercial surveillance system where the floor dominates the frame and everything is foreshortened from above. The image quality is real security footage — washed-out color with visible digital compression banding, luminance noise in the shadow areas, slight motion smear, the flat unflattering look of a cheap 1080p dome camera with a tiny sensor and bad automatic white balance struggling with the overhead fluorescent lighting. Timestamp burned into the lower right reading "CAM 03 02:47:14" in small monospaced white digital text.

Behind the counter of a standard American convenience store — racks of cigarettes on the wall behind her, a lottery ticket display, a slushy machine with two spinning drums of blue and red, shelves of candy and gum at the register, the mundane clutter of a gas station shop at three in the morning. An attractive young white woman in her early twenties works the night shift alone — she has her hair pulled back in a messy ponytail, wearing the store's branded polo shirt and a lanyard with a name badge. The store is completely empty. No customers. No cars visible through the front windows. The parking lot lights cast a pale blue wash through the glass into the otherwise fluorescent-lit interior.

On the counter in front of her, seen clearly from the overhead camera angle, is a giant centipede — roughly two and a half feet long, a real enormous tropical centipede with a segmented dark reddish-brown chitinous body, dozens of pairs of yellow-orange legs rippling along its length, prominent antennae twitching, its body curled in a relaxed S-shape across the countertop between the card reader and the candy rack. It is massive and it is comfortable. It has been here before.

She is feeding it buffalo wings. A small styrofoam takeout container of buffalo wings sits open on the counter beside the register — the wings glistening with orange sauce — and she is holding a single buffalo wing between her fingers and offering it to the centipede's head, her other hand resting casually on the counter, her posture completely relaxed and unbothered, the body language of someone doing something they do every shift. The centipede's mandibles are working on a wing it already has, a previous wing's stripped bone visible on the counter nearby. She is watching it eat with a soft fond expression — not fear, not disgust, the way you watch a pet you are attached to. Her head is tilted slightly. She might be talking to it.

The overhead fluorescent tubes cast the flat shadowless light of a convenience store at night — everything evenly lit in that slightly green-tinged way cheap fluorescents produce, the centipede's chitinous shell catching dull highlights along its segmented back, the buffalo sauce glistening on the wings and faintly on the centipede's mandibles. The store is still and quiet — the slushy machines are the only movement in the background, their drums slowly rotating. Through the front windows the parking lot is empty and dark. Nobody knows this is happening. The framing is the fixed indifferent wide-angle of a surveillance camera that has been recording this every Tuesday night for months and nobody has checked the footage until now. No border, no frame, edge to edge.
label: fake

“Before the First Bell”

Hyper-detailed yet softly painterly intimate fantasy portrait of a lithe elven woman standing on a narrow balcony of a high stone tower just before sunrise. The camera is close—framed from mid-thigh to head—while the vast sky behind her dissolves into soft gradients of deep blue giving way to pale morning light.

She has a lightly athletic build—long limbs, subtle definition through her arms and torso, her posture poised but relaxed as she leans slightly forward over the stone railing. Her skin is pale with a faint cool warmth beginning to touch it, rendered with fine texture—soft pores, delicate tonal shifts along her shoulders and neck.

Her long, dark hair is loosely gathered, strands slipping free and moving gently in the faint morning air. Her expression is quiet and inward—eyes focused on something small and close rather than the horizon beyond.

She is fully clothed in layered, travel-worn garments—soft gray underlayers with a muted blue outer wrap that drapes and folds naturally around her frame. The fabric is fine but weathered, edges slightly frayed, catching the earliest light in soft, matte highlights.

Her hands are mid-action, carefully tying a thin length of cord around a small hanging bell fixed to the balcony’s edge. The motion is delicate and deliberate—fingers working the knot with practiced ease. The bell rests still for now, not yet disturbed.

The faintest magical presence exists in the air itself—barely perceptible, as if the quiet before morning holds a subtle tension, a softness that resists being broken.

The stone railing is worn smooth in places, rough in others, with small cracks and weathering visible. Below, nothing is clearly defined—only soft suggestion of depth and distance.

Lighting is transitional and layered—cool pre-dawn blue dominates the scene, while the first hint of warm sunlight begins to edge along the horizon, catching the outer lines of her form and the fabric’s folds. Highlights remain soft and restrained, shadows deep but not harsh.

Color palette is controlled—deep blues, soft grays, pale skin tones, and the faintest gold beginning to emerge at the edges.

The moment feels suspended at the threshold of change—a quiet preparation before the world begins again, where even the smallest action carries the weight of what comes next.

prompt:
Authentic color CCTV security camera footage from a ceiling-mounted wide-angle dome camera in the corner of a small convenience store, captured at 2:47 AM. The camera is mounted high near the ceiling looking sharply downward at a steep overhead angle across the service counter and front area of the store — the flattened top-down fish-eye distortion of a real commercial surveillance system where the floor dominates the frame and everything is foreshortened from above. The image quality is real security footage — washed-out color with visible digital compression banding, luminance noise in the shadow areas, slight motion smear, the flat unflattering look of a cheap 1080p dome camera with a tiny sensor and bad automatic white balance struggling with the overhead fluorescent lighting. Timestamp burned into the lower right reading "CAM 03 02:47:14" in small monospaced white digital text.

Behind the counter of a standard American convenience store — racks of cigarettes on the wall behind her, a lottery ticket display, a slushy machine with two spinning drums of blue and red, shelves of candy and gum at the register, the mundane clutter of a gas station shop at three in the morning. An attractive young white woman in her early twenties works the night shift alone — she has her hair pulled back in a messy ponytail, wearing the store's branded polo shirt and a lanyard with a name badge. The store is completely empty. No customers. No cars visible through the front windows. The parking lot lights cast a pale blue wash through the glass into the otherwise fluorescent-lit interior.

On the counter in front of her, seen clearly from the overhead camera angle, is a giant centipede — roughly two and a half feet long, a real enormous tropical centipede with a segmented dark reddish-brown chitinous body, dozens of pairs of yellow-orange legs rippling along its length, prominent antennae twitching, its body curled in a relaxed S-shape across the countertop between the card reader and the candy rack. It is massive and it is comfortable. It has been here before.

She is feeding it buffalo wings. A small styrofoam takeout container of buffalo wings sits open on the counter beside the register — the wings glistening with orange sauce — and she is holding a single buffalo wing between her fingers and offering it to the centipede's head, her other hand resting casually on the counter, her posture completely relaxed and unbothered, the body language of someone doing something they do every shift. The centipede's mandibles are working on a wing it already has, a previous wing's stripped bone visible on the counter nearby. She is watching it eat with a soft fond expression — not fear, not disgust, the way you watch a pet you are attached to. Her head is tilted slightly. She might be talking to it.

The overhead fluorescent tubes cast the flat shadowless light of a convenience store at night — everything evenly lit in that slightly green-tinged way cheap fluorescents produce, the centipede's chitinous shell catching dull highlights along its segmented back, the buffalo sauce glistening on the wings and faintly on the centipede's mandibles. The store is still and quiet — the slushy machines are the only movement in the background, their drums slowly rotating. Through the front windows the parking lot is empty and dark. Nobody knows this is happening. The framing is the fixed indifferent wide-angle of a surveillance camera that has been recording this every Tuesday night for months and nobody has checked the footage until now. No border, no frame, edge to edge.

“Before the First Bell”

Hyper-detailed yet softly painterly intimate fantasy portrait of a lithe elven woman standing on a narrow balcony of a high stone tower just before sunrise. The camera is close—framed from mid-thigh to head—while the vast sky behind her dissolves into soft gradients of deep blue giving way to pale morning light.

prompt:
Authentic color CCTV security camera footage from a ceiling-mounted wide-angle dome camera in the corner of a small convenience store, captured at 2:47 AM. The camera is mounted high near the ceiling looking sharply downward at a steep overhead angle across the service counter and front area of the store — the flattened top-down fish-eye distortion of a real commercial surveillance system where the floor dominates the frame and everything is foreshortened from above. The image quality is real security footage — washed-out color with visible digital compression banding, luminance noise in the shadow areas, slight motion smear, the flat unflattering look of a cheap 1080p dome camera with a tiny sensor and bad automatic white balance struggling with the overhead fluorescent lighting. Timestamp burned into the lower right reading "CAM 03 02:47:14" in small monospaced white digital text.

Behind the counter of a standard American convenience store — racks of cigarettes on the wall behind her, a lottery ticket display, a slushy machine with two spinning drums of blue and red, shelves of candy and gum at the register, the mundane clutter of a gas station shop at three in the morning. An attractive young white woman in her early twenties works the night shift alone — she has her hair pulled back in a messy ponytail, wearing the store's branded polo shirt and a lanyard with a name badge. The store is completely empty. No customers. No cars visible through the front windows. The parking lot lights cast a pale blue wash through the glass into the otherwise fluorescent-lit interior.

On the counter in front of her, seen clearly from the overhead camera angle, is a giant centipede — roughly two and a half feet long, a real enormous tropical centipede with a segmented dark reddish-brown chitinous body, dozens of pairs of yellow-orange legs rippling along its length, prominent antennae twitching, its body curled in a relaxed S-shape across the countertop between the card reader and the candy rack. It is massive and it is comfortable. It has been here before.

She is feeding it buffalo wings. A small styrofoam takeout container of buffalo wings sits open on the counter beside the register — the wings glistening with orange sauce — and she is holding a single buffalo wing between her fingers and offering it to the centipede's head, her other hand resting casually on the counter, her posture completely relaxed and unbothered, the body language of someone doing something they do every shift. The centipede's mandibles are working on a wing it already has, a previous wing's stripped bone visible on the counter nearby. She is watching it eat with a soft fond expression — not fear, not disgust, the way you watch a pet you are attached to. Her head is tilted slightly. She might be talking to it.

The overhead fluorescent tubes cast the flat shadowless light of a convenience store at night — everything evenly lit in that slightly green-tinged way cheap fluorescents produce, the centipede's chitinous shell catching dull highlights along its segmented back, the buffalo sauce glistening on the wings and faintly on the centipede's mandibles. The store is still and quiet — the slushy machines are the only movement in the background, their drums slowly rotating. Through the front windows the parking lot is empty and dark. Nobody knows this is happening. The framing is the fixed indifferent wide-angle of a surveillance camera that has been recording this every Tuesday night for months and nobody has checked the footage until now. No border, no frame, edge to edge.

prompt:
“Before the First Bell”

Hyper-detailed yet softly painterly intimate fantasy portrait of a lithe elven woman standing on a narrow balcony of a high stone tower just before sunrise. The camera is close—framed from mid-thigh to head—while the vast sky behind her dissolves into soft gradients of deep blue giving way to pale morning light.

She has a lightly athletic build—long limbs, subtle definition through her arms and torso, her posture poised but relaxed as she leans slightly forward over the stone railing. Her skin is pale with a faint cool warmth beginning to touch it, rendered with fine texture—soft pores, delicate tonal shifts along her shoulders and neck.

Her long, dark hair is loosely gathered, strands slipping free and moving gently in the faint morning air. Her expression is quiet and inward—eyes focused on something small and close rather than the horizon beyond.

She is fully clothed in layered, travel-worn garments—soft gray underlayers with a muted blue outer wrap that drapes and folds naturally around her frame. The fabric is fine but weathered, edges slightly frayed, catching the earliest light in soft, matte highlights.

Her hands are mid-action, carefully tying a thin length of cord around a small hanging bell fixed to the balcony’s edge. The motion is delicate and deliberate—fingers working the knot with practiced ease. The bell rests still for now, not yet disturbed.

The faintest magical presence exists in the air itself—barely perceptible, as if the quiet before morning holds a subtle tension, a softness that resists being broken.

The stone railing is worn smooth in places, rough in others, with small cracks and weathering visible. Below, nothing is clearly defined—only soft suggestion of depth and distance.

Lighting is transitional and layered—cool pre-dawn blue dominates the scene, while the first hint of warm sunlight begins to edge along the horizon, catching the outer lines of her form and the fabric’s folds. Highlights remain soft and restrained, shadows deep but not harsh.

Color palette is controlled—deep blues, soft grays, pale skin tones, and the faintest gold beginning to emerge at the edges.

The moment feels suspended at the threshold of change—a quiet preparation before the world begins again, where even the smallest action carries the weight of what comes next.

A hyper-detailed, cinematic scene featuring a battle-hardened goblin warrior crouched low in a dense, war-torn jungle environment, gripping a massive, heavy rotary machine gun with both hands. The composition is tight and slightly low-angled, placing the viewer at eye level with the goblin and intensifying the sense of confrontation and immediacy. The goblin’s physique is muscular and compact, with thick, sinewy arms and rough, mottled green skin covered in grime, scars, and subtle variations in tone—olive, dark jade, and earthy undertones—suggesting years of brutal combat and survival in harsh environments.

His face is highly expressive and deeply textured, with exaggerated goblin features: a wide, flattened nose, pronounced brow ridges, sharp cheekbones, and large, pointed ears that extend outward. His eyes glow faintly amber-yellow, piercing and focused, conveying aggression, alertness, and a predator-like intensity. His lips curl slightly around a lit cigarette clenched between his teeth, with thin wisps of smoke rising and curling into the humid jungle air. The cigarette ember glows faintly orange, adding a small but vivid point of contrast against the cooler tones of his skin.

He wears a red, worn bandana tied tightly around his head, the fabric faded and slightly dirty, with creases and sweat stains that add realism. Across his bare upper torso runs a heavy bandolier of large-calibre ammunition, the brass casings catching highlights from nearby firelight. His hands are wrapped in rugged, fingerless gloves, dark and scuffed, gripping the weapon with visible tension and strength.

The machine gun is a large, multi-barrel rotary weapon, industrial and imposing, with intricate mechanical detailing—scratches, oil stains, and worn metal edges. Ammunition belts feed into the weapon, draping downward in a heavy curve, each round meticulously rendered. The barrels dominate the foreground, slightly out of focus at the extreme tip, enhancing depth of field and perspective while directing attention toward the goblin’s face.

The background is a chaotic jungle battlefield, dense with tall, dark tree trunks and thick undergrowth. Flames rage behind the goblin, consuming foliage and debris, casting a warm, flickering orange glow that contrasts sharply with the cooler green and blue tones of the jungle. Sparks and embers float through the air, frozen mid-motion, adding energy and dynamism to the scene. Smoke billows upward in thick, semi-transparent plumes, diffusing the light and creating a hazy, cinematic atmosphere.

Lighting is dramatic and high-contrast, with strong directional firelight illuminating one side of the goblin’s face and body, while cooler ambient light from the jungle fills in the shadows. This creates a rich interplay of warm and cool tones, emphasising texture and depth. Subsurface scattering on the skin and subtle specular highlights on sweat and grime enhance realism.

The ground is muddy and uneven, scattered with debris, broken branches, and ash, reinforcing the sense of an active war zone. The goblin’s lower body is partially visible, clad in worn, camouflage-patterned trousers that blend into the environment but are dirtied and torn from use.
